chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Crl.Misc.Appln.No.503 of 2025 Date Order with signature of the Judge Fresh Case 1 For orders on M.A No. 8215 of 2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection a/w reply at Flag-“A” 3. For orders on M.A No. 8216 of 2025 (Ex/Appl) 4. For hearing of main case. 5. For orders on M.A No. 8217 of 2025 (Stay) 05.06.2025 Mr. Nihal Khan, Advocate for the Applicant ************* 1. Urgency granted. 2. In response to the compliance of office objection, learned counsel for the applicant has placed on record certified true copy of the order dated 04.06.2025, which is taken on record. 3. Exemption granted subject to all just exception. 4&5. Learned counsel for the applicant has drawn attention of the Court towards FIR bearing No. 169 of 2025 (available at Page 25 of the Court File) to show that on 21.05.2025, the said FIR was lodged by the Applicant against the Respondent No.1 with respect to an incident Learned counsel further drew attention to the Application under Section 22-A&B, Cr.PC filed by the Respondent No.1 bearing Crl.Misc.Appln. No.1823 of 2025 wherein the incident is shown to be of 25.04.2025, whereas the Application under Section 22-A&B, Cr.PC has been filed after the delay of around twenty days. He further points out in addition to the above, that copies of TCS receipts (available at Page 23 of the Court file) which shows that the TCS was sent on 24.05.2025 at 14:18, whereas to the contrary, the alleged incident is shown to have been occurred at 9:15 P.M which further contradicts the allegation raised by the Respondent No.1; lastly argued that without application of legal mind without and hearing the present Applicant, the impugned order has been passed.

No.1823 of 2025 wherein the incident is shown to be of 25.04.2025, whereas the Application under Section 22-A&B, Cr.PC has been filed after the delay of around twenty days. He further points out in addition to the above, that copies of TCS receipts (available at Page 23 of the Court file) which shows that the TCS was sent on 24.05.2025 at 14:18, whereas to the contrary, the alleged incident is shown to have been occurred at 9:15 P.M which further contradicts the allegation raised by the Respondent No.1; lastly argued that without application of legal mind without and hearing the present Applicant, the impugned order has been passed. The points raised by the learned counsel raise serious questions of law and fact, in view thereof the operation of impugned order dated 04.06.2025 is suspended till the next date of hearing. Let notice be issued to the respondents as well as Prosecutor General, Sindh. Adjourned. To come up on 18.06.2025 J U D G E brohips
